codeReview.summary: The feature meets its stated scope. Commit `dfb6f2b` adds a minimal ESLint 9 flat config at the repo root, enables `@typescript-eslint` plus React Hooks rules, ignores the required generated/backend directories, and the accompanying lint-fix edits in `src/lib/scriptStats.ts` and `src/teleprompter/TeleprompterView.tsx` are narrow and behavior-preserving. I did not find any concrete blocking or non-blocking implementation defects in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This feature was assigned the `frontend-redesign` skill even though it is config-only ESLint work. The worker reasonably skipped that UI-focused procedure, which suggests a skill-assignment gap for non-UI maintenance tasks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\mitte\.factory\missions\fe650437-0d94-4ece-8085-ac6da736d0ec\features.json:364 sets `skillName` to `frontend-redesign` for `fix-eslint-config`; `D:\coding\somewhat\.factory\skills\frontend-redesign\SKILL.md` is scoped to component extraction/redesign/testing; the transcript skeleton for worker session `87958d83-989c-4f0a-8445-2e3790a33767` shows no invocation of that skill, and the handoff file records the deviation as justified because the task was purely config work.

summary: Pass. The ESLint flat-config feature satisfies its description and expected behavior, and I found no actionable code defects in the reviewed commit.
